stories.title: US Women's Open — Kupcho Leads, Korda 7 Back After Round 1
stories.angle: Jennifer Kupcho posts 5-under 66 for Round 1 lead; Sei Young Kim 1 back; Korda shoots 73 (+2), 7 back — changed shoes mid-round (LeBron James custom Nikes)

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
US WOMEN'S OPEN — ROUND 1 FINAL:

No. 1 — Jennifer Kupcho: -5 (66)
No. 2 — Sei Young Kim: -4 (67)
...
Nelly Korda: +2 (73) — 7 back

Kupcho made 7 birdies at Riviera. Korda changed shoes mid-round (LeBron gifted them). Still 54 holes. #USWO #LPGA

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Jennifer Kupcho leads the US Women's Open after Round 1 — 7 birdies, 5-under 66 at Riviera.

Sei Young Kim is one back. Nelly Korda is 7 back after a 73 and a mid-round shoe change.

Korda has come back from worse. But she's got work to do. #LPGA #USWO

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 AM ET

Jennifer Kupcho is setting the early pace at the 81st U.S. Women's Open.

Kupcho birdied seven holes during a confident, composed opening round at Riviera Country Club in Pacific Palisades, posting a 5-under 66 to lead by one stroke over Sei Young Kim, who carded a 4-under 67.

The leaderboard is bunched at the top — that is what Riviera, a demanding classic layout, tends to produce — but Kupcho has the outright lead heading into Round 2, and she looked every bit a major champion playing at the level this course demands.

Meanwhile, Nelly Korda's round was a story all its own. World No. 1, the 2026 season's dominant force, walked to the first tee wearing custom Nike golf shoes personally gifted by LeBron James. By the time she reached hole 16, she had swapped them for a different pair from the locker room. "The fit wasn't right," she explained afterward. "Slightly different spacing."

Korda finished the round at 2-over 73 — seven shots off the lead, outside the top 50. It is not where she wanted to be. But it is also not where this story ends. Korda has 54 holes remaining, and she has spent the better part of 2026 proving that she is the best player on the planet.

The U.S. Women's Open continues June 5-7 at Riviera.
facebook.image_caption: Jennifer Kupcho leads the 2026 US Women's Open after Round 1 — 7 birdies, 5-under 66 at Riviera. Nelly Korda is 7 back. Game on. #USWO #LPGA

article.headline: Jennifer Kupcho Grabs US Women's Open Lead, Korda Seven Back After Shoe-Swapping Round 1

stories.title: Memorial Tournament — Four-Way Tie at the Top, Scheffler Struggles
stories.angle: Fleetwood, Clark, Spaun, Gerard share lead at -5 (67); only 22 under par in tough Muirfield conditions; Scheffler shoots 73 (+1), six back — three-peat bid in early trouble

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
MEMORIAL TOURNAMENT — ROUND 1 FINAL:

Four-way tie at -5 (67):
Fleetwood, Clark, Spaun, Gerard

Only 22 players broke par at Muirfield Village.

Scottie Scheffler: +1 (73) — 6 back. The three-peat bid did NOT start well.

Round 2 underway now. #Memorial #PGATour

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Tommy Fleetwood's Round 1 at the Memorial:

11-of-11 scrambling. ZERO bogeys. 7 greens in regulation.

He scored 67 (-5) by just… never making a mistake.

That's a quiet way to grab a share of the lead at Muirfield Village. #Memorial #PGATour

facebook.long_form:
Window: 10:30 AM ET

Muirfield Village is playing as hard as its reputation suggests.

After the first round of the 2026 Memorial Tournament, just 22 players have broken par at the Jack Nicklaus-designed course in Dublin, Ohio. Four of them share the lead: Tommy Fleetwood, Wyndham Clark, J.J. Spaun, and Ryan Gerard are all at 5-under 67.

Fleetwood's round deserves a separate paragraph. The Englishman hit only seven greens in regulation — a number that would typically suggest a frustrating day — but scrambled brilliantly, converting 11-of-11 up-and-down attempts without recording a single bogey. He scored 67 without any margin for error, finding pars and birdies from positions that other players were three-putting or carding double-bogeys from.

Clark, the 2023 U.S. Open champion, came into the week on the outside of the signature event field until his CJ Cup Byron Nelson victory provided entry. His 67 was his first significant competitive result of the spring.

And then there is Scottie Scheffler. The two-time defending Memorial champion, who came to Dublin seeking what would be only the second three-peat in tournament history — Tiger Woods did it from 1999 to 2001 — shot a 1-over 73 that he called "frustrating." A double-bogey on 16, where the wind switched and sent his ball into the water, was the decisive blow.

Six shots back. Three rounds remaining.
facebook.image_caption: MEMORIAL ROUND 1 LEADERS: Fleetwood, Clark, Spaun, Gerard — all at -5. Scheffler fires 73 (+1), 6 back. Muirfield Village is playing tough. #Memorial #PGATour

article.headline: Memorial Tournament Round 1: Fleetwood, Clark, Spaun, Gerard Share Lead as Scheffler Struggles

stories.title: LIV Andalucia — Gooch Surges to 4-Shot Lead After Round 2
stories.angle: Talor Gooch shoots 66 in Round 2, leads by 4 over Dustin Johnson; final round Saturday; at iconic Valderrama; Gooch seeking second Andalucia title

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
LIV Andalucia R2 at Valderrama:

Talor Gooch shoots 66 — leads by 4 over Dustin Johnson.

Final round is Saturday. Open Championship spot goes to the winner.

Gooch won here in 2023. He wants to win here again. #LIVGolf #Valderrama

facebook.long_form:
Window: 1:00 PM ET

Talor Gooch has played Valderrama like he owns it.

After a second-round 66 at Real Club Valderrama in Sotogrande, Spain, Gooch leads LIV Golf Andalucia by four strokes over Dustin Johnson heading into Saturday's final round. Gooch, the captain of OKGC, won the LIV Andalucia in 2023 at the same venue, and he has clearly reconnected with the course's demanding, tree-lined fairways and small greens.

The story at Valderrama this week includes more than just Gooch. Jon Rahm — the Spanish legend who captains Legion XIII — is chasing a home-circuit victory that would mean everything in this country. DeChambeau and several others remain within range of the leaders heading into the final round.

One additional incentive awaits the winner: an invitation to the Open Championship at Royal Birkdale. LIV Golf's deal with The Open has made the winner's spot a genuine prize, and for players who have watched traditional leaderboards from the outside in recent years, that invitation carries real weight.

Final round is Saturday at Valderrama.
facebook.image_caption: Talor Gooch leads LIV Andalucia by 4 shots after Round 2 at Valderrama. Open Championship spot for the winner. Final round Saturday. 🏆 #LIVGolf

article.headline: Gooch Grabs Four-Shot Lead After Round 2 at LIV Golf Andalucia

stories.title: Scheffler's Three-Peat Bid in Trouble — Six Back After Round 1 Memorial
stories.angle: Scheffler fired 73 — "frustrating" 1-over round; 6 shots behind; would need to match Tiger's only three-peat in Memorial history (1999-2001); bouncing-back mode

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Scottie Scheffler's Memorial three-peat bid after Round 1:

+1. 73. "Frustrating." Six shots back.

Double bogey on 16 — wind shifted, ball in water.

Tiger's three-peat (1999-2001) is still the only one. Scheffler's path just got harder.

#Memorial #PGATour

facebook.long_form:
Window: 1:00 PM ET

Scottie Scheffler is not one to make excuses, so his characterization of Thursday's opening round at the Memorial Tournament as "frustrating" means something.

Scheffler, the two-time defending champion who arrived at Muirfield Village seeking what would be only the second three-peat in Memorial Tournament history, shot a 1-over 73. He is six shots behind the four co-leaders. He has three rounds to make it up.

The round went sideways on the 16th hole, where a wind change sent his tee shot into the water for a double bogey. He added a bogey on 14. The rest of the round was the Scheffler that everyone expects — quality ball-striking, intelligent course management — but six shots in the wrong direction does not disappear because the subsequent holes were fine.

The precedent Scheffler is chasing is daunting even without the round-one deficit. Tiger Woods won this event in 1999, 2000, and 2001 — the only three-peat in Memorial history. Woods won the tournament five times total. Scheffler won in 2024 (8-under, one shot over Morikawa) and 2025 (10-under, four shots over Ben Griffin). A 2026 victory would write his name alongside Tiger in a very specific part of the record book.

The math is still possible. Six shots at Muirfield Village over three rounds is not insurmountable for the world's best player. But Thursday did not go according to plan.
facebook.image_caption: Scheffler fires 73 in Round 1 at the Memorial — 6 back of leaders. He needs Tiger's three-peat (1999-2001). The path just got harder. ⛳ #Memorial #Scheffler

article.headline: Scheffler's Memorial Three-Peat Bid Takes a Hit After Frustrating Round 1

stories.title: Korda's LeBron Shoe Swap — A Quirky Moment in a Tough Round 1
stories.angle: Korda swapped LeBron James' custom Nike shoes mid-round (hole 16) because fit wasn't right; carded 73; still has 54 holes to make US Women's Open history; human-interest angle

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Nelly Korda wore LeBron James' custom Nike golf shoes to start the US Women's Open.

She changed them on hole 16.

"The fit wasn't right. Slightly different spacing."

She shot 73. She's 7 back. She has 54 holes left.

Not the way Korda planned her USWO week. #LPGA #USWO #Korda

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:30 PM ET

There is a version of this story where Nelly Korda wore LeBron James' custom Nikes and shot 66. That version is not what happened.

What happened: Korda, the world's top-ranked women's golfer and the most dominant player on the LPGA Tour in 2026, walked to the first tee at Riviera Country Club wearing a pair of custom Nike golf shoes personally gifted to her by LeBron James. They looked great. They did not feel right.

By hole 16, Korda had sent someone to the locker room for a different pair. "The fit wasn't right," she said after her round. "Slightly different spacing." The custom version — in a different color than her usual pair — had just enough of a difference in construction that it affected her feel through the course.

Korda finished the round at 2-over 73. She is seven shots behind first-round leader Jennifer Kupcho. She is outside the top 50 in the tournament she came here to win.

But this is Nelly Korda in 2026. She has three wins, 17 career LPGA titles, and a Chevron Championship that serves as her third major. She has been the best player on the planet all season. One round, even a bad one, is not the end of this story.

She will put on the right shoes tomorrow. And then we will see if Riviera gets a comeback worth watching. 👟⛳
facebook.image_caption: Nelly Korda wore LeBron James' custom shoes to start the US Women's Open. Changed them on hole 16. Shot 73. Seven back. Still has 54 holes. #LPGA #USWO #Korda

article.headline: Korda Swapped LeBron's Custom Shoes Mid-Round at the US Women's Open. Then Shot 73.
